actions.title: Draft AFTA 2026 conference abstract submission
actions.plan:
1. Research AFTA 2026 call for proposals timeline and format requirements
2. Draft a 250-word abstract positioning FamilyDiagram's training app as 'AI-assisted assessment skill development for family therapy students'
3. Frame around SARF coding scheme + F1 metrics as objective skill measurement
4. Save to btcopilot/doc/marketing/afta-2026-abstract.md for Patrick's review and editing
actions.spawn_prompt:
Research the AFTA (American Family Therapy Academy) conference abstract submission process. Their 2025 theme was 'Systemic Therapy, Research, and Education in the Age of Technology and AI' — check if 2026 call for proposals is open yet.

Then draft a conference abstract (250-word max) for FamilyDiagram's training application. Key points to include:
- Family therapy students practice clinical interviews with AI-generated personas based on synthetic family system scenarios
- The SARF clinical coding scheme (Symptom, Anxiety, Relationship, Functioning) provides structured assessment of student extraction quality
- F1 metrics compare student extractions to expert-coded ground truth, providing objective skill measurement
- Preliminary results: People extraction F1=0.72, Events F1=0.29 (AI baseline), showing the metric discriminates between extraction quality levels
- Implications for standardized clinical assessment training

Write the abstract in academic conference style. Save to /Users/hurin/Projects/theapp/btcopilot/doc/marketing/afta-2026-abstract.md (create the marketing directory if needed).

Also draft a short list of 3-4 Bowen theory training programs to contact for beta testing, with rationale for each:
- The Bowen Center (Georgetown)
- Kansas City Family Systems Center
- Center for Family Consultation (Evanston, IL)
- ISSFI (international)

Save contact research to /Users/hurin/Projects/theapp/btcopilot/doc/marketing/training-program-outreach.md.

Acceptance criteria: Two markdown files created with polished, ready-to-edit content.
actions.success_metric: Abstract draft and outreach list ready for Patrick's review

actions.title: Create competitive comparison matrix document
actions.plan:
1. Create a detailed feature comparison matrix: FamilyDiagram vs GenoPro vs Genogram Analytics vs Qwoach vs Creately
2. Include: clinical data model, AI features, collaboration, platforms, pricing, HIPAA, Bowen theory support
3. Save to btcopilot/doc/marketing/competitive-matrix.md
actions.spawn_prompt:
Create a competitive comparison matrix for FamilyDiagram vs its competitors. Save to /Users/hurin/Projects/theapp/btcopilot/doc/marketing/competitive-matrix.md (create marketing directory if needed).

Compare these products across these dimensions:

**Products:**
1. FamilyDiagram (our product)
2. GenoPro / GenoProX
3. Genogram Analytics
4. Qwoach / EasyGenogram
5. Creately (genogram templates)
6. SimplePractice (practice management with basic genogram)

**Dimensions (create a markdown table):**
- Clinical data model (none / basic genogram / SARF-level)
- AI extraction from conversation (yes/no)
- Bowen theory framework (none / symbols only / full SARF coding)
- Timeline visualization (none / basic / event+variable tracking)
- Ground truth validation pipeline (yes/no)
- Platform (Windows / Mac / Web / Mobile)
- Real-time collaboration (yes/no)
- HIPAA compliance (stated / BAA available / N/A)
- Pricing (monthly/annual)
- Primary target user
- Key strength
- Key weakness

For FamilyDiagram, reference the actual codebase features:
- SARF clinical coding (Symptom, Anxiety, Relationship, Functioning variables)
- AI single-prompt extraction with PDP accept/reject workflow
- F1 metric validation against expert ground truth
- Desktop (PyQt5) + Mobile (QML) + Training Web App
- Professional plan: $19.99/mo or $199.99/yr
- Stripe subscription management

Also add a 'Positioning Summary' section below the table explaining why FamilyDiagram is in a category of one (clinical assessment system vs diagramming tool).

Acceptance criteria: Clean markdown table with accurate competitive data, suitable for internal strategy use.
actions.success_metric: Competitive matrix document created with accurate feature comparisons
